candidates.name: Alan Mark Grayson
candidates.bio: Alan Mark Grayson (born March 13, 1958) is an American politician  who served as the U.S. representative for Florida's 8th congressional district from 2009 to 2011 and Florida's 9th congressional district from 2013 to 2017. A member of the Democratic Party, he was defeated for reelection in 2010 by Republican Daniel Webster; he was then reelected in 2012 for a second, non-consecutive term in the U.S. House of Representatives in another district, defeating Republican Todd Long.
key_messages: Grayson supports improving financial literacy among Americans through government education programs, financial institutions, media, and school-based financial education to help citizens manage money and understand economic matters.

Grayson advocates for making healthcare more affordable by comparing insurance plans, utilizing employer-provided plans, and budgeting carefully to reduce costs while maintaining adequate health coverage.

Grayson supports making college accessible through multiple funding sources including federal aid with flexible repayment plans, state funding, university scholarships, and private grants to ensure no American skips college due to financial troubles.
Grayson opposes endless war and war profiteering based on his public messaging.

campaign_themes.text: Grayson supports improving financial literacy among Americans through government education programs, financial institutions, media, and school-based financial education to help citizens manage money and understand economic matters.

campaign_themes.text: Grayson opposes endless war and war profiteering based on his public messaging.

platform_excerpts.excerpt: The government is involved in educating about the economy... We introduce financial education in schools and colleges. From an early age, our children learn about salaries, prices, and even taxes.
Look at insurance plans. Compare the options of bronze, silver, gold, and platinum insurance... Make sure you pay for the services you need... look at what your employer can provide you.

The government encourages higher education. Therefore, it provides flexible repayment plans... there are plenty of options to get your child through college... No American should skip college because of financial troubles!
Grayson on Endless War and War Profiteering

top_stances.summary: Make college accessible through federal aid with flexible repayment, state funding, scholarships, and grants.

candidates.name: Jennifer Jenkins
candidates.bio: Jennifer Jenkins, FAcSS is a British linguist and academic. She was Chair Professor of Global Englishes at the University of Southampton until her retirement in 2019. She is a leading figure in the study of English as a Lingua Franca (ELF), and is an expert on communication in English between non-native speakers. This is a relatively new field of study and her opinions are  sometimes seen as controversial. Her interests include attitudes to the international range of "Englishes", English as a lingua franca in higher education, and the implications of ELF for ways of teaching English as a forei

candidates.name: Bernard Korn
candidates.bio: The 2024 United States Senate election in Florida was held on November 5, 2024, to elect a member of the United States Senate to represent the state of Florida. Incumbent Republican Senator Rick Scott won a second term, defeating Democratic nominee Debbie Mucarsel-Powell. The primary election was held on August 20, 2024.

candidates.name: Angela Nixon
candidates.bio: Angela Nixon (born February 27, 1984) is an American politician who has served as a Democratic member in the Florida House of Representatives since 2020. She is a candidate in the 2026 United States Senate special election in Florida.
key_messages: Nixon supports cracking down on corporate price-gouging through federal legislation and breaking up corporate consolidation and monopolies driving up costs.
Nixon supports Medicare for All as a universal, single-payer system and will use federal power to cap and negotiate prescription drug costs.
Nixon supports fully funding public education, ending voucher schemes, investing in Title I funding, and raising educator salaries.
Nixon believes housing is a right and supports funding and building affordable housing at scale with down-payment assistance to prevent displacement.
Nixon supports real police accountability legislation and protecting the rights of Black communities without compromise.
Nixon opposes AIPAC influence and special interest control of U.S. foreign policy, asserting that foreign policy must answer to the American people.

campaign_themes.heading: economy
campaign_themes.text: Nixon supports cracking down on corporate price-gouging through federal legislation and breaking up corporate consolidation and monopolies driving up costs.

campaign_themes.heading: healthcare
campaign_themes.text: Nixon supports Medicare for All as a universal, single-payer system and will use federal power to cap and negotiate prescription drug costs.

campaign_themes.heading: education
campaign_themes.text: Nixon supports fully funding public education, ending voucher schemes, investing in Title I funding, and raising educator salaries.

campaign_themes.heading: housing
campaign_themes.text: Nixon believes housing is a right and supports funding and building affordable housing at scale with down-payment assistance to prevent displacement.

campaign_themes.heading: criminal_justice
campaign_themes.text: Nixon supports real police accountability legislation and protecting the rights of Black communities without compromise.

campaign_themes.heading: foreign_policy
campaign_themes.text: Nixon opposes AIPAC influence and special interest control of U.S. foreign policy, asserting that foreign policy must answer to the American people.

platform_excerpts.excerpt: We will pass and enforce federal legislation to stop corporations from inflating prices on groceries, fuel, and essential goods to pad their bottom line... We will take on the corporate consolidation and monopolies that are driving up costs across every sector of daily life.

Medicare for All: Healthcare is a right. Angie will fight for a universal, single-payer system. No more insurance companies standing between you and your doctor. Lower Drug Prices Now: Use federal power to cap and negotiate prescription drug costs.

Fully Fund Public Education: End the diversion of public dollars into private voucher schemes. We will increase Title I funding and invest where the need is greatest... Raise salaries, reduce burnout, and guarantee mental health resources in every school.
Housing is a Right: We will fund and build affordable housing at scale, expand down-payment assistance, and stop the displacement of working families from the communities they built.
Defend Civil Rights: Pass real police accountability legislation and protect the rights of our Black communities, immigrant and LGBTQ+ neighbors without compromise.

No AIPAC or Special Interest Control: Angie will not take money from AIPAC or any organization that expects to shape U.S. policy without accountability to the people she represents. Foreign policy must answer to the American people—not to a lobbying machine.

top_stances.summary: Crack down on corporate price-gouging through federal legislation and break up monopolies driving up costs.
top_stances.track_record_note: Healthcare & Pharma industry contributed $6,550 to campaign despite stated opposition to corporate consolidation and price-gouging in pharma sector.

top_stances.summary: Support Medicare for All universal single-payer system and use federal power to cap and negotiate prescription drug costs.
top_stances.track_record_note: Healthcare & Pharma industry contributed $6,550 to campaign despite stated support for aggressive prescription drug cost negotiation and single-payer model.

top_stances.summary: Fully fund public education, end voucher schemes, invest in Title I funding, and raise educator salaries.
Housing is a right; fund and build affordable housing at scale with down-payment assistance to prevent displacement.
Enact real police accountability legislation and protect the rights of Black communities without compromise.
Oppose AIPAC influence and special interest control of U.S. foreign policy; ensure foreign policy answers to American people.

candidates.name: Dennis Gene Mr Stevens
candidates.bio: Dennis Stevens (30 November 1933 – 20 December 2012) was an English footballer, born in Dudley, Worcestershire who played in the Football League for Bolton Wanderers, Everton, Oldham Athletic and Tranmere Rovers.

candidates.name: Alexander Vindman
candidates.bio: Alexander Semyon Vindman (born Aleksandr Semyonovich Vindman,[a] June 6, 1975) is a retired United States Army lieutenant colonel who was the Director of European Affairs for the United States National Security Council (NSC) until he was reassigned on February 7, 2020. He came to national attention in October 2019 as a key witness before the United States Congress regarding the Trump–Ukraine scandal. His testimony revealed that he, himself, had triggered the House inquiry, and provided evidence that resulted in a charge of abuse of power in the first impeachment of Donald Trump.

candidates.name: Joshua Weil
candidates.bio: The 2025 Florida's 6th congressional district special election was held on April 1, 2025, to choose a new member of the U.S. House of Representatives. The seat became vacant following the resignation of Republican incumbent Mike Waltz, who in 2024 had been re-elected with 66.5% of the vote, but was chosen by President Donald Trump to be his White House national security advisor. The district is considered safely Republican.
